docs.description: Modal dialog with compound component architecture. Uses box-drawing title decoration, side borders, and native dialog element.

notes.title: Compound Architecture
notes.content: Dialog is composed of 11 parts: Dialog, DialogTrigger, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogBody, DialogFooter, DialogClose, DialogAction, and DialogKeyboardHints.

notes.title: Native Dialog
notes.content: DialogContent uses the native <dialog> element with showModal(), which provides built-in focus management, inert background, top-layer rendering, and accessible modal semantics. Focus is restored to the trigger on close.

notes.title: Alert Dialog
notes.content: Set role="alertdialog" and closeOnBackdropClick={false} on DialogContent for destructive confirmations. Screen readers announce it as an alert requiring immediate attention. Per WAI-ARIA APG, alert dialogs should not close on outside interaction, and focus should start on the safest action (e.g., Cancel) using autoFocus.

notes.title: Escape Interception
notes.content: Pass onEscapeKeyDown to DialogContent to intercept the Escape key. Call e.preventDefault() to keep the dialog open — useful during async operations or form validation.

notes.title: Preventing Close on Action
notes.content: DialogAction and DialogClose check e.defaultPrevented. Call e.preventDefault() in your onClick handler to keep the dialog open — useful for async validation where you want to close only on success.

notes.title: DialogKeyboardHints
notes.content: A sub-component that renders keyboard shortcut hints below the dialog. Accepts custom hints via the hints prop. Returns null for an empty hints array.

keyboard.description: Dialog uses the native <dialog> element for modal behavior. Escape closes the dialog and focus is automatically restored to the trigger. Tab cycles focus between focusable elements within the dialog (native inert background). Enter activates the focused button (DialogAction or DialogClose).
